title: Q2 renewal forecast lands within 2% of committed ARR
anchor: Actual renewal ARR at $1.77M versus forecast of $1.8M, a 1.7% variance on $1.8M renewing book.
connect: Trailing four-quarter forecast variance averaged 8.2%, making Q2 the tightest forecast in recent quarters.
body: Actual renewal ARR at $1.77M versus forecast of $1.8M, a 1.7% variance on $1.8M renewing book. Trailing four-quarter forecast variance averaged 8.2%, making Q2 the tightest forecast in recent quarters.
trace: Q2 renewal forecast accuracy significantly improved over trailing pattern, suggesting better renewal visibility or late-stage deal management.

title: Mid-market NRR jumps 7 points above trailing three-quarter average
anchor: Mid-market NRR at 112% in Q2 versus trailing three-quarter average of 104.7%.
connect: Multi-product mid-market customers drive most expansion momentum at 124% NRR versus single-product at 102%.
body: Mid-market NRR at 112% in Q2 versus trailing three-quarter average of 104.7%. Multi-product mid-market customers drive most expansion momentum at 124% NRR versus single-product at 102%.
trace: Mid-market segment showing strong expansion performance, particularly when Atlas Insights is in the stack.

title: Multi-product customers expand at 22-point NRR premium over single-product
anchor: Multi-product customers at 124% NRR across 46 accounts versus single-product at 102% across 74 accounts in Q2.
connect: Multi-product share sits at 38.3% of the customer base currently.
body: Multi-product customers at 124% NRR across 46 accounts versus single-product at 102% across 74 accounts in Q2. Multi-product share sits at 38.3% of the customer base currently.
trace: Clear NRR advantage for multi-product accounts, with room to grow penetration from current 38% share.

title: At-risk pool shrinks $90K month-over-month with 4 accounts clearing
anchor: April at-risk ARR at $220K across 3 accounts versus March at $310K across 7 accounts.
connect: Top-20-ARR at-risk concentration drops from 5 accounts in March to 2 in April.
body: April at-risk ARR at $220K across 3 accounts versus March at $310K across 7 accounts. Top-20-ARR at-risk concentration drops from 5 accounts in March to 2 in April.
trace: Risk pool reducing both in dollar terms and account count, with improvement in high-value account risk profile.

title: High-touch tier retention holds 14-point advantage over tech-touch
anchor: High-touch GRR at 96% in Q2 versus tech-touch at 82% across coverage tiers.
connect: High-touch covers 22 accounts representing 18.3% of customer base, concentrated in top ARR bands.
body: High-touch GRR at 96% in Q2 versus tech-touch at 82% across coverage tiers. High-touch covers 22 accounts representing 18.3% of customer base, concentrated in top ARR bands.
trace: Coverage model showing expected retention differential, with premium tier protecting highest-value accounts.

title: Mid-market time to first value compresses 15 days quarter-over-quarter
anchor: Q2 mid-market TTFV at 23 days across 24 accounts versus Q1 at 38 days across 22 accounts.
connect: Q1 cohort under-30-day TTFV renewed at 91% versus over-60-day at 74%.
body: Q2 mid-market TTFV at 23 days across 24 accounts versus Q1 at 38 days across 22 accounts. Q1 cohort under-30-day TTFV renewed at 91% versus over-60-day at 74%.
trace: Onboarding velocity improving with clear retention correlation for faster time-to-value cohorts.

body: March customer health reviews sourced 6 expansion opportunities, sales accepted 5 for 83% acceptance rate. Outbound expansion opportunities convert at 41% sales acceptance across 17 sourced in March.
